summary.intent: Package the Gossamer Electron app for distribution via Homebrew, including bundling the server inside the app and automating releases via GitHub Actions.
summary.outcome: A working Homebrew Cask was published for v1.0.7 with correct SHA256s, a GitHub Actions release pipeline was set up, and the server is bundled inside the Electron app via extraResources. Several CI issues were resolved iteratively.

repo: The project has two separate package.json files: root (gossamer-serve server) and app/ (Electron frontend) — they were originally independent programs.
server.ts uses import.meta.url to resolve PROJECT_ROOT and find Prisma binaries, so bundled layout must preserve the relative path structure.
prisma was in devDependencies but is needed at runtime for pushSchema — must be in dependencies.
utilityProcess.fork() requires a CJS entry point; the server is ESM, requiring a CJS wrapper (server-launcher.cjs) that dynamically import()s the ESM server.
The app/ subdirectory is a git worktree child of the root repo, which caused electron-builder to fail to detect the GitHub repository via .git/config.
The homebrew tap repo must be named homebrew-gossamer and cask files must live in a Casks/ subdirectory.
DMG filenames are determined by the version in app/package.json, not the git tag — these can diverge and cause 404s in cask download URLs.

code.finding: Added electron-builder, extraResources config to bundle server/, and a repository field (required by electron-builder to detect GitHub for publish config).
Modified to spawn gossamer-serve as a utilityProcess in production (gated on absence of ELECTRON_RENDERER_URL), leaving dev workflow unchanged.
CJS bridge that dynamically import()s the ESM server entry — required because utilityProcess.fork() only supports CJS entry points.

Must include permissions: contents: write at the job level — GitHub Actions defaults to read-only, which prevents release creation. Also must pass --publish never to electron-builder to avoid it trying to publish directly (softprops step handles upload).
Used as a sed template in CI to generate the updated cask — heredocs inside YAML run: | blocks break YAML parsing and must be avoided.

workflow: electron-builder's --publish flag must be set to 'never' when a separate upload step (softprops/action-gh-release) is used, otherwise it tries to publish itself and fails if not on a tag.
Fine-grained GitHub PATs for updating a tap repo need only Contents: Read and write on the target repo.
Heredocs inside YAML run: | blocks cause silent syntax errors — use sed substitution against a committed template file instead.
npm ci will fail if package-lock.json is out of sync with package.json after adding new dependencies; always run npm install locally and commit the updated lock file.
GitHub Actions GITHUB_TOKEN requires explicit permissions: contents: write to create releases; the default is read-only.

friction: Multiple CI iteration cycles due to lock file being out of sync after adding electron-builder.
electron-builder failed to detect GitHub repo because .git is in the parent of app/ — required adding an explicit repository field to app/package.json.
DMG filenames used app/package.json version, not the git tag, causing a mismatch between the cask URL and actual release assets for v1.0.7.
The cask auto-update step in the workflow never ran correctly for v1.0.7 — SHA256s had to be computed and committed manually.
A heredoc in the workflow YAML broke the pipeline and required a refactor to use a sed-based template approach.
Network outage mid-session delayed pushing several commits.
Worktree confusion: a local worktree for the README branch checked out the local tip instead of origin/README, causing apparent history mismatch.
open_items: Consolidating app/ and root into a single package was discussed and explicitly deferred — the separate folder structure creates ongoing friction with extraResources using ../ paths.
The app is unsigned (no Apple Developer cert) — users must manually allow it via System Settings on first launch. Requires $99/yr Apple Developer account to fix.
The branch trigger in release.yml was changed to README for testing and then reverted — confirm the tag-only trigger is correct in the final state.
The workflow version-sync step (setting app/package.json version from the git tag before building) was added but not yet validated end-to-end with a new release.
The gossamer-serve server could optionally still be released as a standalone CLI artifact for headless use, but this was not implemented.
